summary.intent: Remove the automatic model probe from provider validation to fix timeout/model-loading churn issues reported by users with local LLM servers (LemonadeSDK, Ollama), and replace it with an optional per-provider 'Test' button in the UI.
summary.outcome: Successfully removed the probe loop from validate_key (returning valid immediately after /models discovery), added a per-provider 'Test' button with confirmation dialog reusing the existing models.test RPC, updated all button text/i18n across en/fr/zh locales, fixed E2E tests, removed dead code (reorder_models_for_validation, probe_priority_rank), and rebuilt Tailwind CSS. All 90 provider-setup tests pass.

repo: Provider validation probe sends actual POST /chat/completions with 'ping' per model, forcing local LLM servers to load each model into VRAM — VALIDATION_MAX_MODEL_PROBES=8 means up to 8 model load/unload cycles just to save a provider.
models.test RPC already exists for single-model probing — the frontend has testModel() in provider-validation.js that can be reused for the optional test button.

chat_error.rs has structured error parsing covering auth failures, timeouts, connection refused, model not found, rate limits — chat-time errors are already well-surfaced in the UI, making the probe redundant for UX purposes.
reorder_models_for_validation() and probe_priority_rank() functions existed to sort models for probe order (small models first) — now removed as dead code after probe loop deletion.
Tailwind CSS must be rebuilt when new arbitrary value classes are added (e.g., text-[var(--success,#22c55e)]) — build is in crates/web/ui.
Biome is used for JS linting/formatting across the web crates.
The project uses a 'beads' issue tracker (not GitHub issues) — issues are created and claimed via CLI before starting implementation work.
Worktrees are used for feature branches: path pattern is /Users/penso/.superset/worktrees/moltis/<branch-name>/.

code.finding: The removed probe loop — iterated up to VALIDATION_MAX_MODEL_PROBES=8 models, calling probe_chat_completions() on each, breaking on first success or accumulating timeouts up to MAX_TIMEOUTS=1.
probe_chat_completions() sends actual chat completion request with 'ping' message — forces model load on local servers.
Ollama path with selected_model: when model is found in discovered list, falls through (no early return) to the main validation flow — now returns model list directly instead of probing.

workflow: When removing a feature, check for dead code (helper functions, tests) that only existed to support it — removing cleanly avoids future confusion.
E2E test button text references must be updated when UI button labels change — grep for exact button text strings across spec files.
Rust unused function warnings surface dead code after logic changes — treat them as a checklist for cleanup.
cargo clippy failures on unrelated crates (llama-cpp-sys cmake build) can be bypassed by running clippy on the specific crate being changed.
Test names should reflect current behavior — rename tests when their name references removed behavior (e.g., 'probe_uses_v1_endpoint' after removing probing).

friction: Tailwind CSS rebuild location was not immediately obvious — required searching for tailwind config files before finding the correct crates/web/ui directory.
cargo clippy on the full workspace failed due to an unrelated llama-cpp-sys cmake build issue, requiring fallback to crate-specific clippy runs.
The GitHub discussion fetch required two attempts (gh CLI then GraphQL API) before succeeding.

'Save & Validate' string was scattered across providers.js, onboarding-view.js, three locale files (en/fr/zh) for both providers and onboarding namespaces, and two E2E spec files — required multiple grep passes to find all instances.
open_items: The 'validating' i18n key was updated to 'Saving...' but the provider-validation-progress.js hint text infrastructure remains — it may be vestigial if the save flow no longer shows progress UI.

The optional 'Test' button confirmation dialog warns about token usage and model loading, but there's no mechanism to select which model gets tested — it uses whatever testModel() picks by default, which may still trigger a large model load on servers like LemonadeSDK.
French and Chinese translations for new keys (test, testing, testSuccess, testFailed, testProviderConfirm) were added but may need review by native speakers.

The probe infrastructure (probe() trait method, probe_chat_completions()) is retained in the provider implementations — could be cleaned up if the manual test button path is confirmed to not need it long-term.
No consideration given to whether the 'Detect All Models' feature (models.detect_supported) also has the same model-loading churn problem for local servers.
